---
title: atmos vendor verify
sidebar_label: verify
sidebar_class_name: command
id: vendor-verify
description: Verify vendored files match vendor.lock.yaml, reporting drift for CI.
---
import Intro from '@site/src/components/Intro'

<Intro>
Compare every lock-owned file on disk against its recorded `vendor.lock.yaml` receipt and
report drift — missing files, or files whose contents no longer match what was last vendored.
Exits non-zero when any drift is found, for use in CI.
</Intro>

## Usage

```shell
atmos vendor verify [--component <name>] [--format table|json]
```

This never checks for a newer version upstream — see
<a href="/cli/commands/vendor/update">`atmos vendor update --check`</a> for that. `vendor verify`
is purely a read-only, on-disk integrity check against what's already recorded in the lock file:
it never fetches, writes, or modifies anything.

## Examples

```shell
# Verify every vendored artifact.
atmos vendor verify

# Verify only one component.
atmos vendor verify --component vpc

# Machine-readable output for CI.
atmos vendor verify --format json
```

## Flags

<dl>
  <dt>`--component` / `-c` (string)</dt>
  <dd>Verify only this component. Omit to verify every artifact recorded in the lock file.</dd>
  <dt>`--format` (string, default `table`)</dt>
  <dd>Output format: `table` or `json`.</dd>
</dl>

:::note
`atmos vendor verify` reports drift; it never fixes it. Re-run `atmos vendor pull --refresh-lock`
(or `atmos vendor pull --component <name> --refresh-lock` for a single component) to re-fetch and
reconcile drifted sources.
:::
